title: Personal Information
shortSummary: Talk about who you are: your name, age, nationality, job, and where you live.
culturalNote: Germans tend to keep a clear line between private and public life, so deep personal questions come later in a friendship than they might elsewhere. Job and city are normal small talk; salary and relationship status are not. When stating your profession, Germans drop the article: 'Ich bin Lehrer' (I am a teacher), with no 'ein'.

keyPhrases.context: Note: no article before the profession

grammar.explanation: When you say what you do, German omits the indefinite article. 'Ich bin Arzt' (I am a doctor), not 'Ich bin ein Arzt'. The same applies to nationalities and religions.
grammar.commonMistake: Adding ein: it is 'Ich bin Lehrer', not 'Ich bin ein Lehrer'.

grammar.rule: aus vs in for origin and residence
grammar.explanation: Use 'aus' to say where you are originally from, and 'in' to say where you live now. 'Ich komme aus Spanien, aber ich wohne in Berlin.'
grammar.commonMistake: Mixing them up: 'Ich komme in Berlin' is wrong; origin uses aus.

dialogue.setup: At a language exchange evening, Tom introduces himself to Sofia.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.german: Hallo, ich heiße Tom. Wie heißt du?
lines.english: Hi, I'm Tom. What's your name?

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.english: I'm Sofia. Nice to meet you!

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.german: Ich komme aus Spanien, aber ich wohne jetzt in Köln.
lines.english: I'm from Spain, but I live in Cologne now.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.german: Schön! Und was machst du beruflich?
lines.english: Nice! And what do you do for work?

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.german: Ich bin Student. Ich studiere Informatik.
lines.english: I'm a student. I study computer science.

quiz.question: How do you say 'I come from Australia'?
quiz.explanation: Origin uses 'aus' plus the country name.

quiz.question: Ich _____ in Berlin. (I live in Berlin)
quiz.explanation: wohnen means to live/reside; ich wohne is the ich form.

quiz.question: Which sentence is correct?
quiz.explanation: German drops the article before professions.

quiz.question: Which word means 'married'?
quiz.explanation: verheiratet means married; ledig means single.

quiz.question: Arrange: heiße / ich / Sofia
quiz.explanation: Subject ich, then verb heiße, then the name.
